user.title: Connect to your EGOptimizer brain
user.description: EGOptimizer decides how much solar energy to feed into your energy community without ever risking your own autarky. Home Assistant sends live state to the brain and shows its recommendation. Your battery's minimum SoC stays enforced by Victron — the brain only ever spends energy above your morning target.

data_description.brain_url: Address of the brain container, e.g. http://192.168.1.50:8787.
data_description.capacity_kwh: Your battery's usable capacity. Used to convert percentages to kWh.
data_description.soc_entity: Current battery charge in %. Required — this drives every calculation.
data_description.load_entity: Live household consumption (W). Prefer a smoothed/averaged sensor so a kettle switching on doesn't jerk the plan. The brain uses it to estimate overnight load.
data_description.solcast_entity: Solcast 'Prognose heute' sensor (e.g. sensor.solcast_pv_forecast_forecast_today). The brain reads its hourly 'detailedHourly' attribute and takes the pessimistic P10 estimate per hour — P10 because under-promising on PV keeps your autarky safe.
data_description.solcast_tomorrow_entity: Solcast 'Prognose morgen' sensor (e.g. ..._forecast_tomorrow). The brain ALWAYS needs tomorrow too — the overnight low point falls tomorrow morning, where the recharge curve lives. You can usually leave this blank: if your today sensor is named ..._today, the matching ..._tomorrow is detected automatically. Set it only if your naming differs.
data_description.hard_min_entity: Optional: your Victron minimum-SoC number. Used only to clamp the target — the brain never controls SoC itself.
data_description.scan_minutes: How often Home Assistant re-asks the brain. Every recompute adapts to the latest SoC, load and forecast.

abort.already_configured: This brain URL is already configured.

upload_data.title: Upload a CSV export
upload_data.description: Upload your grid-operator export (e.g. NetzNÖ 'Jahreseinspeisung'). The brain imports it (de-duplicating, dropping meter glitches) and learns from it — no folders needed. Tick 'upload another' to add more files in a row; the model retrains once you finish. Files uploaded so far this session: {count}.

data.upload_another: Upload another file after this one

data_description.file: A .csv export. The original filename matters — your metering-point id is read from it.
data_description.upload_another: Leave on to keep adding files; turn off to finish and retrain.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Connection, battery and the entities the brain reads. When to feed is decided automatically by what the community absorbs (learned) and your battery simulation — there is no fixed time window to set.

data_description.capacity_kwh: Your battery's usable capacity in kWh.
data_description.load_entity: Smoothed household consumption (W). The plan adapts to it every recompute.
data_description.solcast_tomorrow_entity: Solcast 'Prognose morgen' sensor. Combined with today so the forward curve always reaches tomorrow's morning recharge; past hours ignored.
data_description.hard_min_entity: Optional Victron minimum-SoC number, used only to clamp the target.
data_description.retention_days: Default retention used by 'Delete old data'. 0 keeps everything.

delete_data.title: Delete old data
delete_data.description: Permanently remove imported intervals older than the retention period from the brain's database. This cannot be undone. The learning model is unaffected until its next retrain.

data_description.retention_days: Everything older than this many days is deleted. 0 would delete everything — use with care.
data_description.confirm: You must tick this to proceed.

error.cannot_connect: Couldn't reach the brain. Check the URL and that the container is running.
error.upload_failed: Couldn't read the uploaded file. Try again.
error.not_confirmed: Tick the confirm box to delete data.

import_csv.name: Import CSV
import_csv.description: Upload a grid-operator CSV export to the brain (alternative to the UI).

path.name: File path
path.description: Path to a CSV readable by Home Assistant.

content.name: CSV content
content.description: Or paste the raw CSV text.

filename.name: Filename
filename.description: Original export filename (metering-point id is read from it).

train.name: Train model
train.description: Refit the absorption model from all imported data.
